title: San Francisco Botanical Garden | Hours, Tickets & Info | GGGP
content: Explore 55 beautiful acres of landscaped gardens and open spaces that showcase more than 8,000 different kinds of plants from around the world. Andean Cloud Forest Australia California Chile Mediterranean Mesoamerican Cloud Forest New Zealand South Africa Southeast Asia Cloud Forest Temperate Asia. Ancient Plants Moon Viewing Garden Children’s Garden Conifer Lawn Celebration Garden Fountain Plaza Garden of Fragrance Great Meadow Succulent Garden Zellerbach Garden. San Francisco’s cool foggy climate is reminiscent of these highland regions in the tropics, so it is no wonder that our Andean Cloud Forest Collection has flourished. One of our most striking features are the Andean wax palms (*Ceroxylon quindiuense*), the highest occurring and tallest species of palm in the world. There is no denying the beauty and diversity of California’s landscapes, and as a result, its flora is the most diverse in the United States at over 6000 species.
